editionData.title: Daily Brief for April 27, 2026: Global Headlines, Markets, Hacker News, Product Hunt
editionData.description: A daily brief for April 27, 2026 covering global headlines, market closes, Hacker News, and Product Hunt.
editionData.author: Sony Mathew

headlines.title: U.S. Mint Buys Drug Cartel Gold and Sells It as ‘American’
headlines.summary: As prices for the precious metal soar, the industry’s guardrails have broken down.

headlines.title: Israel’s President, Putting Off Decision on Pardon for Netanyahu, Will Push for Plea Deal
headlines.summary: President Isaac Herzog of Israel has decided not to issue a pardon to Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu in his corruption case at this time, and instead will seek mediation, officials say.

headlines.title: Iran and U.S. Sink Into Awkward Limbo of ‘No War, No Peace’
headlines.summary: Each side is betting it can last longer than the other, analysts say. But there are risks in a stalemate without a deal.

headlines.title: Israel and Hezbollah Trade Strikes in Lebanon, as Iran Talks Remain on Hold
headlines.summary: Cease-fires in Lebanon and Iran are on shaky ground, with military attacks flaring and direct talks between Washington and Tehran to end their war stalled.

headlines.title: What Happened After The New York Times Found a Cartel Mine on a Colombian Military Base
headlines.summary: Officers denied that an illegal, large-scale gold operation was underway within earshot of their posts. But we had seen it with our own eyes.

headlines.title: Iran’s Araghchi to meet Russia’s Putin; Israel kills 14 in Lebanon
Iran’s foreign minister leaves Pakistan, heads to Russia for more talks
Former Israeli PMs Bennett, Lapid unite to challenge Netanyahu in elections
Israel and Hezbollah trade blame as evacuation orders signal escalation
Accused shooter was targeting Trump and US officials, authorities say
Alleged Colorado attacker's family released after nearly a year in detention
headlines.summary: A Texas judge ordered Hayam El Gamal and her five children released Thursday. Two days later, their lawyers say, ICE re-arrested and tried to deport them.

headlines.title: A fast-growing Georgia wildfire tops 31 square miles, with evacuations possible
headlines.summary: One of two large wildfires in southeastern Georgia continues to grow and now exceeds 31 square miles.

headlines.title: A chaotic White House Correspondents' Dinner, as told by NPR reporters in the room
headlines.summary: Top government officials were rushed out of the Washington Hilton after gunshots were heard at the dinner. NPR journalists describe hiding under tables and scrambling for information as the night unfolded.

headlines.title: What we know about Cole Allen, suspected White House Correspondents' dinner shooter
headlines.summary: The 31-year-old teacher and engineer from California sent an alleged message to family members saying that he wanted to target administration officials.

headlines.title: Photos: The aftermath of the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting
headlines.summary: A suspect has been arrested after firing shots at a security screening area at the Washington Hilton on Saturday night.

headlines.title: Ukraine marks 40 years of Chernobyl disaster
headlines.summary: SLAVUTYCH, Ukraine — People streamed into the central square of Slavutych in the early hours of Sunday, placing candles on a large radiation hazard symbol laid out on the ground as a midnight commemoration began for those killed in the Chernobyl disaster 40 years ago and the thousands who risked deadly radiation exposure to contain its aftermath.

headlines.title: US shooting bares security vulnerabilities
headlines.summary: WASHINGTON — The shooting on Saturday night of a Secret Service agent at the White House Correspondents' Association dinner raises questions yet again about security at a time of increased political violence.

headlines.title: Germany, France push for Hormuz navigation
headlines.summary: As Germany prepares to send naval ships to the Mediterranean Sea, French President Emmanuel Macron has renewed his push to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, amid a warning from the chief of energy company TotalEnergies that a prolonged Iran war could trigger global energy shortages.

headlines.title: King 'should meet Epstein survivors' in US
headlines.summary: Britain's King Charles III has been urged to meet the survivors of Jeffrey Epstein's sexual abuse during his visit to the United States this week.

headlines.title: African leaders call for tackling growth hurdles
headlines.summary: As Africa pushes to industrialize its economies, Uganda's President Yoweri Museveni has urged the continent to draw on China's experience, saying discipline, ideological clarity, governance and a strong internal market are key to economic transformation.

headlines.title: BJP attacking Bengali culture, pride: Kejriwal; Modi won’t have his way in Bengal: Tejashwi
headlines.summary: Arvind Kejriwal and Tejaswi Yadav join Trinamool Congress campaign in West Bengal amid tensions with Congress and BJP.

headlines.title: NSA Doval discusses regional situation with UAE President Al Nahyan
headlines.summary: NSA Ajit Doval meets UAE President Al Nahyan to discuss enhancing strategic partnership and regional issues of mutual interest.

headlines.title: Rahul Gandhi plans Great Nicobar visit, clearance awaited
headlines.summary: Rahul Gandhi's Great Nicobar visit awaits clearance amid concerns over tribal displacement from a proposed ₹92,000-crore infrastructure project.

headlines.title: Rajasthan may serve as “living lab” for farm tech companies: CM Bhajan Lal Sharma
headlines.summary: Rajasthan aims to be a "living lab" for agri-tech, offering diverse investment opportunities to transform its agriculture sector.

headlines.title: SIR pause on way to a billion electorate
headlines.summary: The SIR has trimmed India’s electoral roll by removing absent, shifted, dead, and duplicate names, leading to record turnout percentages across States despite shrunken electorates; while the exercise strengthens the accuracy of the roll, the challenge now is to ensure it remains inclusive and free of disenfranchisement

headlines.title: Buckingham Palace says King Charles' trip to the US to go ahead

Mali Defence Minister Sadio Camara killed in attack on Saturday, state TV reports
